verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein that is a member of the immunoglobulin superfamily. It is expressed specifically in sperm cells. In immature/epididymal sperm, IZUMO1 is sequestered intracellularly (in the acrosomal membrane facing the interior). Upon acrosome reaction (triggered at fertilization), IZUMO1 relocates to the plasma membrane outer leaflet of the sperm head, where it is essential for sperm-egg fusion by binding its oocyte receptor JUNO. This surface display is therefore restricted to: (1) a narrow germline/reproductive cell type (sperm), and (2) a defined activation state (post-acrosome reaction). The protein has its own TM anchor but expression and surface exposure are restricted to this narrow biological context. This fits 'contextual' / 'tissue_restricted_surface' (germline/gamete-restricted display with its own TM anchor, activated-state requirement).
